posts.text: The mods of the r/BlakeLivelyFans subreddit asked members of their community to post messages of support for Blake Lively as she continues her sexual harassment case. What you’re seeing here is a small collage of those messages, of people taking a moment to remind someone under an enormous amount of scrutiny that she isn’t alone. Public accusations of harassment invite doubt, hostility, and the exhausting demand that survivors constantly prove their own credibility. Under that immense pressure,

Melissa Nathan and Breanna Butler Koslow are both mentioned in Jeffrey Epstein's emails as being connected to firms providing PR and other services for him.    Which is largely unsurprising given the history of The Agency Group's members and their willingness to work alongside deeply problematic individuals to aid them in smearing, discrediting and ruining the reputations of those speaking out against their clients.    What is surprising is that even knowing Melissa Nathan's dark history and her

@Molls  and I chat through the newly unsealed evidence in the Blake Lively v Justin Baldoni lawsuit, Russian baths, Jameela Jamil and the harassment of Taylor Swift in order to isolate Blake from her friends. The full chat is up on Patreon right now at even the $1 level, so please go show your support. Link in bio. #blakelively #justinbaldoni #itendswithus #popculture #feminism

For a year, Justin Baldoni's main counter to the accusations of sexual harassment and inappropriate conduct from multiple women on the set of IT Ends With Us has been that Blake Lively stole Justin's movie. Newly unsealed evidence shows that Baldoni's contract with Sony meant he never had control of the movie and was always in competition with Sony's cut. A competition his cut lost, never hitting the scores he agreed to. Rather than admit failure, he sought to blame everyone around him, just as

In his deposition testimony Justin Baldoni admits he broke strike rules, rewrote the script during the writer's strike and pressured cast and crew to work during the strike.   While claiming on his Man Enough podcast that he stood in solidarity with the unions.  Much in the same way Baldoni accepted his award for championing women's voices, while trying to silence, bully and threaten the women who spoke up against him. All of which highlights the difficulty and frustration with trusting men who

In attempting to cover up their own misconduct, Justin Baldoni and Wayfarer have targeted the women who have spoken up about their negative experiences with him. Going so far as to allegedly file false claims in a court of law. Baldoni may not have harassed and abused all of them, but he has stood by watching and encouraging the attacks on their careers in order to save his own. #blakelively #justinbaldoni #itendswithus #popculture #feminism

It's been almost a year since Justin Baldoni was accused of sexual harassment and a hostile work environment. In that time, cast, producers and crew all state in deposition testimony under oath, that Baldoni's inappropriate behavior began long before the cameras started rolling and never stopped. Far from this being about Blake Lively, multiple women in multiple departments from multiple Wayfarer productions have all witnessed a pattern of dismissive, inappropriate behavior and verbal abuse from

So much of what is marketed to men as self-improvement is a grueling regime of punishment, suffering and pain in order to transform yourself into someone else: A man who learns that discipline and neglect are more important than listening to your needs.  When in reality, those who thrive are the people who learn to examine the unhealed parts of themselves, to sit with their wounded self and learn which of their unmet needs causes them to live in anger, loneliness, shame and fear. Then learn to f

There are still scores of men who view marriage as a system by which they "provide" in order to rent a woman's body.  Fully acknowledging they want to return to coercive and controlling relationships whereby men provide money, in return for women's labor and intimacy, never once understanding why it might be that a 60 year long movement has sought to liberate women from this. Yet that system, they acknowledge, lacks the affection, appreciation and love they yearn for. Because control can't coexi

Stitch with @theofficialkatya og  It is not surprising that newly revealed texts show that Wayfarer's leadership first tried to bully and intimidate the women raising concerns on set about their behavior. Nor is it surprising that once that behavior become public knowledge, Baldoni worked to avoid accountability, triangulate and instead frame the allegations as an attack, that he was the true victim. All of this is the predictable playbook of abusive personalities when their behavior is called o
